Free-form message scoring — no DB lookups, no auth-context
mutation. Pass whatever you have; missing fields contribute 0

Canonical 5-level severity used everywhere a finding has a
severity. Matches CVSS-derived industry convention.

on threat-intel internals.

Push a batch of pre-shaped events to a SIEM connector. The dict

SourceAuthConfig.title: SourceAuthConfig

track.

get.description:
Unified feed of recent agentic runs. Pulls from every agent's
table, normalises onto :class:`AgentActivityItem`, sorts by
``created_at`` desc, returns the top ``limit``.

Cheap implementation — fetch ``limit`` rows from each table and
merge in Python. Tables hold ≪ 1M rows in any plausible
deployment so the cost is fine; switch to a UNION ALL view if
that ever changes.

schema.title: Response List Activity Api V1 Agents Activity Get

get.description:
Read the global guard state. Always synchronous — derives from
env vars only. Useful as a banner on the agent pages.

schema.title: Response List Alerts Api V1 Alerts  Get

get.description: Full-text search across alert titles and summaries, scoped to the system organisation.

schema.title: Response Search Alerts Api V1 Alerts Search Get

get.description:
Rank candidate threat actors for this alert with confidence
breakdowns (P2 #2.9). Pure read-only — no DB writes.

Download a MITRE ATT&CK Navigator v4.5 layer for this alert.

Combines (a) every ``AttackTechniqueAttachment`` row on the alert
with (b) the curated ``known_ttps`` of every threat actor sighted
on the alert. The layer's per-square comments preserve provenance
so the analyst sees which agent / actor / rule attached each
technique.

List assets for an organization with rich filtering.

Filters compose with AND semantics. ``q`` is a case-insensitive
substring match against ``value``. Paginated — see ``X-Total-Count``
response header (Audit B6).

schema.title: Response List Assets Api V1 Assets Get

post.description: Create a single asset under an organization.

Bulk-create assets from a JSON payload.

Idempotent on (organization_id, asset_type, value): duplicates are
counted as ``skipped_duplicates`` rather than failing the batch.
Per-row validation errors are collected in ``errors``; the rest of
the batch still commits.

Bulk-import assets from a CSV file.

Required columns: ``asset_type``, ``value``.
Optional columns: ``criticality``, ``tags`` (semicolon-separated),
``details_json`` (JSON-encoded string).

get.description: Return per-type and per-criticality counts for the org's asset registry.

Return JSON-schema for the ``details`` field of every asset type.

Used by the dashboard to render type-aware forms.
List audit logs with filters (admin only).

Stream a ZIP archive of SOC2-relevant evidence: audit log,
current user roster (email-hashed), retention policies,
notification channel inventory (no secrets), evidence-vault
counts, and a metadata blob (git sha + version + window).
Auditors get one download per control review.

Each member of the bundle is plain JSON / NDJSON so an auditor
can grep without unpacking custom tooling.
Audit activity statistics: daily counts, top users, action breakdown (admin only).

post.description:
Verify the first TOTP code from the user's authenticator. On
success this marks the account as enrolled (login now requires a
code) and returns 10 single-use recovery codes — shown once,
stored hashed.

Disable MFA. Requires the current password *and* a valid TOTP
code or recovery code. Both checks are needed so a stolen session
token alone cannot strip 2FA.

Begin MFA enrollment. Generates a fresh TOTP secret and returns
the otpauth:// URL the dashboard renders as a QR code. The secret
is stored immediately but ``mfa_enrolled_at`` stays NULL until the
user confirms via ``/2fa/confirm``. Until then the login flow does
*not* require a code, so a half-enrolled user can still get in.
Authenticate with email and password, receive JWT tokens.

Log out (records audit event). Clears auth cookies and asks
legacy clients to discard their tokens.

get.description: Get the current authenticated user's profile.

patch.description: Update the current user's display name or password.

post.description:
Exchange a refresh token for a new access token.

Audit D-1 — refresh token may be sent in the request body (legacy
clients) OR via the ``argus_refresh_token`` HttpOnly cookie that
the browser sets after /auth/login. Cookie wins when both are
present so an attacker who captures a stale body token can't
reuse it after a forced rotation.

Register a new user.

If no users exist yet, the first user is automatically promoted to admin
(bootstrap mode). Otherwise, this endpoint requires an admin bearer token.

schema.title: Response List Brand Actions Api V1 Brand Actions Get

post.description:
Manually file the agent's takedown recommendation as a real
``TakedownTicket``. The agent does NOT auto-submit — this endpoint
is the analyst's deliberate go-button.

Refuses unless:
  * the action is in status ``completed``
  * the recommendation is ``takedown_now`` or ``takedown_after_review``

Idempotent — calling a second time returns the existing ticket id.

Queue a Brand Defender run for a suspect domain.

Idempotent: returns an existing queued/running run for the same
suspect rather than starting a duplicate.

Ingest a batch of newly-registered / observed domains from any
feed (CertStream, WhoisDS, manual). Matches each against the org's
active brand terms; creates SuspectDomain rows for hits.

schema.title: Response List Logos Api V1 Brand Logos Get

post.description:
Register a brand logo. Image bytes go to evidence vault; perceptual
hashes are computed and stored for cheap SQL-side lookups.

Compare an uploaded candidate image (e.g. logo cropped from a

this org. Persists a LogoMatch row for each verdict ≠ no_match.

Computes SHA-256 of the bytes, dedupes within the tenant, stores in

Hard delete is performed by the retention worker per policy.

Useful for browsers behind strict CSPs that can't follow presigned URLs
to a different origin. For large blobs prefer ``/download``.

schema.title: Response List Actor Playbooks Api V1 Intel Actor Playbooks Get

get.description: Surface Atomic Red Team + Caldera config state to the dashboard.

post.description:
Start a Caldera operation. Admin-gated — this triggers attacker
behaviour on customer endpoints, so analysts can't kick it off.

Compute a per-technique coverage report from executed / detected
technique tallies. Pure compute — no DB writes.

get.description: List every breach-credential provider + config state (P3 #3.9).

post.description: Fan-out email lookup across configured breach providers.

HIBP k-anonymity password lookup. Only the first 5 chars of the
SHA-1 leave Argus.

Per-analyst rate-limited: an authenticated analyst can probe at most
20 SHA-1 hashes per minute. Without that gate a malicious / compromised
analyst could iterate through a corporate password list using their
API token (k-anonymity protects HIBP, not us — they still learn whether
any given password hash is breached).

Run CIRCL hashlookup / Passive DNS / Passive SSL on the supplied
IOC and return the classification + records. pDNS and Passive SSL
require ARGUS_CIRCL_USERNAME / _PASSWORD; the wrappers no-op
silently when creds are absent so the route remains usable for
hash lookups against the anonymous endpoint.

schema.title: Response List Cves Api V1 Intel Cves Get

post.description:
Run the curated CISA-Decider-style classifier (P2 #2.2) against
arbitrary text and return ranked technique hits with confidence
scores and the keywords that matched. Pure compute, analyst-gated.

get.description: List every EDR connector + config state (P3 #3.2).

post.description:
Admin-gated — pushes IOCs into the customer's EDR vendor
(CrowdStrike / SentinelOne / MDE) blocklist where they may
auto-block / quarantine endpoints. C6: this is a vendor-side
production change, not analyst-grade.

Admin-gated — host isolation kicks an endpoint off the network
(CrowdStrike contain / S1 disconnect / MDE isolate). High-blast
radius; C6 says analysts cannot trigger this.

get.description: List every email-gateway connector + config state (P3 #3.3).

post.description:
Admin-gated — pushes a deny-list entry into the customer's mail
gateway, which is a production-impacting change.

get.description:
Report which IR-workbench tools are available in this deployment
(P3 #3.11).

post.description:
Admin-gated — schedules a Velociraptor artifact (VQL) on a live
customer endpoint. Same blast-radius class as Caldera operations,
so we mirror that gating.

Admin-gated — shells out to the host's vol3 binary against an
arbitrary absolute file path supplied by the caller. Allowing an
analyst to drive subprocess + arbitrary file-read of host paths is
a C6 escalation; admin gating is the floor.

schema.title: Response List Hardening Api V1 Intel Hardening Get

get.description:
Report whether the Kestrel CLI / module is available in this
deployment so the dashboard can grey out the "Run hunt" button
when only the script-rendering path is usable.

post.description:
Run a Kestrel hunt script. Returns ``available=False`` when
Kestrel is not installed; the analyst can still copy the script.

Compose a Kestrel hunt script — pure function, works regardless
of whether Kestrel is installed. The output script is the durable
case artefact.

get.description: List MISP events updated in the last N days. Optional tag filter.

Fetch a STIX entity's neighbourhood from OpenCTI for the graph
view. Returns ``{root, nodes, edges, note}`` even when OpenCTI is
unavailable so the dashboard renders an explanatory empty state.

post.description: Project an IOC into the co-deployed OpenCTI as a STIX Indicator.

Score an email/message against the Arabic-phishing analyzer.

Returns the full scoreboard (homoglyphs, bidi-overrides,
mixed-script domains, GCC pretexts, impersonated brands) so the
dashboard can render each signal individually. Pure compute — no
DB or external calls — so admin gating is intentionally not required.

get.description: List every sandbox connector + config state (P3 #3.6).

post.description:
Admin-gated — uploads the customer's binary to an external
sandbox vendor (CAPE / Joe / Hybrid-Analysis / VirusTotal).
Sensitive files leaving the tenant boundary is a C6-class
decision — analysts request, admins approve + submit.

get.description: List every SIEM connector + its configuration state (P2 #2.7).
List the SIEM-dialect backends available in this deployment.

post.description: Build a Sigma rule from an IOC and translate to every backend.
Build a Sigma rule from a MITRE technique and translate.
Translate an arbitrary Sigma YAML rule to every backend.

get.description: List every SOAR connector + its configuration state (P3 #3.7).

post.description:
Admin-gated — creates incidents in the customer's SOAR (XSOAR /
Tines / Splunk SOAR), which fans out to other downstream systems.
Analysts can read; only admins push.
One-shot IOC → STIX pattern → per-module translations.

get.description: List the stix-shifter data-source modules importable in this deployment.

post.description:
Translate a STIX 2.x pattern into native query language for every
available data-source module.

stix-shifter spawns its own event loop internally, so we run the
sync entry point inside ``asyncio.to_thread`` to avoid clashing
with FastAPI's running loop ("event loop is already running").

schema.title: Response List Intel Syncs Api V1 Intel Syncs Get

Compile + scan once. For repeated scans against the same rules

Turn a completed investigation into a Case.

schema.title: Response Trigger Cleanup Api V1 Retention Cleanup Post

post.description:
Flip the ``legal_hold`` boolean on any of the legal-holdable
tables. While true, retention refuses to delete the row even after
the configured window has elapsed.

Admin only — the flip is itself audit-logged with before/after.

get.description: Show what would be cleaned up: counts and date ranges by table.

delete.description: Delete a retention policy. Falls back to global default.

post.description: Check for common misconfigurations and exposures.

Discover subdomains for an organization's domains.

Returns ``scan_status``:

* ``ok`` — every passive source completed cleanly. Empty
  ``subdomains`` means the org genuinely has no public subdomain
  footprint.
* ``partial`` — at least one passive source failed (rate-limit,
  DNS, network). The ``errors`` array enumerates which. The
  analyst is told the result is incomplete so an empty list is
  not mis-read as "we're clean".
* ``failed`` — every passive source failed. No usable signal.

get.description: List all users (admin only).

post.description:
Hard-delete a user record and break PII linkage in derivative
tables. The audit log retains the action+timestamp via the
``users.id → audit_logs.user_id`` ON DELETE SET NULL constraint —
we keep the *fact* of every operation, but the actor pointer
becomes anonymous.

The caller logs an audit row before the deletion completes, so
there is always a paper trail of *who* invoked GDPR erasure on
*whom* (recorded by user_id and an SHA-256 of the original email
so future audits can correlate without holding the email itself).

delete.description: Soft-delete a user by setting is_active=False (admin only).

get.description: Get user detail (admin only).

patch.description: Update user role, active status, or display name (admin only).

get.description: List API keys for a user (admin or own account).

delete.description: Revoke an API key (admin or own account).

get.description: Deep health check — verifies database connectivity.
TAXII 2.1 discovery resource (§4.1).
API root resource (§4.2).

List collections — one per tenant org. Argus is single-tenant
on-prem so this returns exactly one row in v1.
Single-collection descriptor (§5.2).

Stream the collection's STIX 2.1 indicators (§5.4).

``?added_after=<RFC 3339>`` lets subscribers fetch only the diff
since their last poll. ``limit`` caps the envelope size. The
response advertises ``X-TAXII-Date-Added-First/Last`` so clients
can paginate without reading the body.
